channel.title: RSS Aggregator Feed
channel.description: An aggregated feed of Technology-related news

item.title: Cisco tried using AI to write security incident reports — and things didn't really go as planned
item.description: There are many challenges with generating long-form reports with AI, even with specific sources and instructions.

item.title: 'We wanted to make a new and unique story, where fans will recognize moments throughout as they're playing' — TT Games on creating a new adventure and how it squeezed decades of superhero history into Lego Batman: Legacy of the Dark Knight
item.description: The Caped Crusader’s singular legend has been rebuilt in a captivating Gothic style by the UK-based studio, who have an authentic hand in this franchise’s legacy.

item.title: Cheap Garmin alert! The Garmin Forerunner 165 is getting discounted thanks to it's successor being announced, and it's a perfect summer running watch pick
item.description: The 4.5-star Garmin Forerunner 165 is $50 cheaper in both standard and Music formats.

item.title: This free email security scanner pairs perfectly with Gmail or Outlook
item.description: See if any email in your inbox is safe or suspicious in seconds. Last summer, I stumbled onto a brilliantly useful tool that ended up being one of the most well-received discoveries we’ve ever shared in these quarters.

item.title: How to watch Bayern Munich vs. VfB Stuttgart online for free
item.description: How to watch DFB-Pokal final for free. Live stream Bayern Munich vs. VfB Stuttgart in the 2026 DFB-Pokal final for free.

item.title: Memorial Day Tech Deals: Sony, Apple, Beats (2026)
item.description: Lots of our most-recommended headphones, power banks, and other gadgets are on sale for Memorial Day.

item.title: AI is wreaking havoc at Starbucks and Pizza Hut. Social media is having a field day
item.description: Starbucks retired an AI inventory tool after frequent mistakes, while Pizza Hut’s delivery system allegedly lost a franchisee more than $100 million in sales. AI woes are coming for the food service industry, and social media can’t help but celebrate.

item.title: 'You have no way to revoke it faster or confirm when it stops working': Experts find Google API keys are still usable, even after you delete them
item.description: For more than 20 minutes after deletion, some Google API keys can still be used, apparently creating a major security gap.

item.title: AI’s real test in education is outcomes
item.description: As AI spreads through classrooms, the only question that matters is whether it strengthens or shortcuts learning. Generative AI arrived in education everywhere all at once. Today’s students are surrounded by tools that promise help, answers, and efficiency at every turn. But learning has never been about convenience alone. As AI reshapes how students engage with academic material, the questions are whether it is being built to support how humans actually learn and ultimately improve outcomes....

item.title: Tesla recalls thousands of Model Y SUVs over missing sticker
item.description: Another week, another Tesla recall, this time centered around the lack of a weight certification label.

item.title: Travelers just ranked the best airlines in America and one major carrier slipped badly
item.description: Travelers increasingly say reliability, WiFi, rewards programs, and customer service matter more than cheap fares alone. Delta Air Lines has become the new go-to carrier that American travelers consider when purchasing a flight, leapfrogging rivals American Airlines and United Airlines, according to an annual study released this week by YouGov.

item.title: How Focus Features and Curry Barker thought outside the box to make horror fans obsessed with ‘Obsession’
item.description: On a tiny budget, the movie scored big at the box office with help from a campaign that leaned into real-world objects and unhinged billboards. Focus Features wants to make people obsessed with its newest horror movie—and it seems to be working.

item.title: AI data centers are making cities warmer. Researchers calculated the exact impact
item.description: Scientists have been sounding the alarm on just how bad artificial intelligence is for the environment, and a new study offers more proof. Scientists have been sounding the alarm on just how bad AI, and data centers, are for the environment. And now, researchers have proof they are also making nearby neighborhoods 4 degrees warmer, according to a new study from Arizona State University....

item.title: “Honestly? We’re genuinely terrified” — Flipper Zero creators reveal massive new Flipper One swiss-army knife PC and ask community for help
item.description: The Flipper One is much more than just an incremental upgrade to the Flipper Zero, offering a Linux kernel, a fully open source platform, and greater modularity than its predecessor.

item.title: Why agencies are giving AI a seat in their org chart
item.description: AI’s had its intern era, and some companies are now promoting it to full-time. I recently talked to a marketer whose company added Claude to their org chart. Not as a joke, but as a real role, with defined responsibilities and a clear place in the workflow.

item.title: NASA is opening up bids for who will run the Jet Propulsion Laboratory
item.description: The JPL's current contract with Caltech ends in 2028.

item.title: Twelve South’s AirFly Pro 2 has hit one of its best prices ahead of summer travel
item.description: With Memorial day weekend kicking off the travel season, we’re seeing a lot of deals pop up on travel gadgets, from portable power banks to noise-canceling headphones. One of the best right now is Twelve South’s AirFly Pro 2 Bluetooth adapter, which lets you use your wireless headphones with in-flight entertainment systems so you can […]
